mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2021-01-01 indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200,000 persons in one month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,631 thousand on 2021-01-01 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market healing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances (below baseline 65.5%), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in retail sales and housing starts signals accelerating consumer and residential demand that boosts corporate revenues and profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand signals to strengthen simultaneously to overcome sectoral noise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST rises > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 540,915 million and HOUST at 1,669 thousand on 2020-12-01
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with housing recovery
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below the prior 3-month average signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.7244 on 2020-12-01 reflects pandemic supply disruption
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines exceed baseline returns by more than 1%, or Rank IC is positive and exceeds 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3% on 2021-01-01 reflects pandemic labor slack
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 in absolute value over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (below 1%) combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPI > 2.5% year-over-year) signals monetary policy lag that creates real rate compression, boosting equity valuations relative to bonds and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 262.231 on 2021-01-01
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances despite real rate compression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration above 2% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. This mechanism is always-on and does not require a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,780.325 billion chained 2017 dollars on 2020-10-01 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (Rank IC < 0.02), or positive GDP growth periods produce negative SPX returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic pessimism, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,669 thousand on 2020-12-01 reflects strong post-pandemic housing demand
regime_hint: housing market weakness or demand destruction
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 in absolute value over any 12-month rolling window.
